Title: xCatalog - Search Products Query
Preconditions: GraphQL endpoint accessible at {{BACK_URL}}; products in catalog
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{BACK_URL}}/graphql
[ACT] Execute: query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" keyword: "laptop") { totalCount items { id name code imgSrc } } }
[WAIT] Wait for response
[ASSERT] Verify response structure
Assertions:
[STATE] data.products object present in response
[STATE] totalCount > 0 for valid keyword
[STATE] items[] non-empty; each item contains id, name, code
[FORMAT] imgSrc is a valid URL or null
[STATE] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 status on POST /graphql
[CONSOLE] No JS errors
[API] Response time < 500ms
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; totalCount = 0 for known keyword; missing id/name/code on items; HTTP non-200

Title: xCatalog - Get Product Detail Query
Preconditions: Product ID known from GQL-001; {{BACK_URL}} accessible
Test_Data: storeId={{STORE_ID}}; productId from GQL-001 result; currencyCode="USD"
Assertions:
[STATE] product object returned with correct id
[STATE] descriptions[] non-empty; content populated
[MATH] prices.list.amount > 0
[STATE] prices.sale present (may be null if no sale active)
[STATE] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200; response time < 500ms
[API] Price hierarchy matches BL-GQL-003 (sale ≥ list validation)
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; product null; descriptions empty; prices missing list amount

Title: xCatalog - Category Tree Query

[WAIT] Wait for response

Assertions:

[STATE] childCategories nested correctly under parent
[FORMAT] Each category has slug (non-empty string suitable for URL)
[MATH] totalCount matches actual items[] length
[STATE] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200; response time < 500ms
[API] Category count matches Admin catalog configuration
[CONSOLE] No JS errors
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; totalCount = 0; slug null or empty; childCategories missing on known parent categories

Title: xCatalog - Product Facets/Filters Query
Preconditions: Products with properties configured; {{BACK_URL}} accessible
Test_Data: storeId={{STORE_ID}}; facetQuery includes brand/price range; filter combinations
Steps:
[ACT] Execute products query with facet parameter: query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" facetQuery: "brand") { totalCount items { id name } facets { name type values { label count } } } }
[WAIT] Wait for response
[ASSERT] Verify aggregations
[ACT] Apply filter and re-query with term facet
[ASSERT] Verify filtered results match applied filter
Assertions:
[STATE] facets[] non-empty; at minimum brand and price range facets present
[STATE] Each facet value has label and count > 0
[STATE] Filtered result set is smaller than unfiltered set
[STATE] Combining two filters uses AND logic (result set narrows further)
[STATE] No errors[] in response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 on each query
[API] Filter counts match product count in filtered response
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; facets[] empty when facet-enabled properties exist; filter does not narrow results; facet count mismatches

Title: xCart - Add Item to Cart Mutation
Preconditions: Cart created from GQL-005; product ID known; user authenticated
Test_Data: cartId from GQL-005; productId={{TEST_SKU}}; quantity=2; authToken={{USER_TOKEN}}
Steps:
[ACT] Execute: mutation { addItem(cartId: "<CART_ID>" productId: "{{TEST_SKU}}" quantity: 2) { id itemsCount items { id productId quantity listPrice { amount } } } }
[WAIT] Wait for response
[ASSERT] Verify errors[] field present and empty
[ASSERT] Verify item added with correct quantity and price
Assertions:
[STATE] errors[] present and empty (BL-GQL-001)
[STATE] itemsCount = 1 (or incremented by 1 if cart had items)
[STATE] items[] contains entry with productId = {{TEST_SKU}}
[STATE] items[0].quantity = 2
[MATH] items[0].listPrice.amount > 0
[STATE] No mixed pricing anomalies
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200
[API] Cart query confirms item presence
[ADMIN] Line item visible in Admin cart view
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; itemsCount not incremented; quantity wrong; listPrice.amount = 0 or null; productId mismatch
Cleanup: remove item from cart via removeCartItem mutation

Title: xCart - Update Cart Item Quantity
Preconditions: Cart with items from GQL-006; lineItemId known; user authenticated
Test_Data: cartId from GQL-005; lineItemId from GQL-006; newQuantity=5; authToken={{USER_TOKEN}}
Steps:
[ACT] Execute: mutation { changeCartItemQuantity(cartId: "<CART_ID>" lineItemId: "<LINE_ITEM_ID>" quantity: 5) { id items { id quantity } totals { subTotal { amount } } } }
[WAIT] Wait for response
[ASSERT] Verify errors[] present and empty
[ASSERT] Verify quantity and totals updated
Assertions:
[STATE] errors[] present and empty (BL-GQL-001)
[STATE] items[0].quantity = 5
[MATH] totals.subTotal.amount = items[0].listPrice.amount × 5 (within rounding tolerance)
[STATE] No stale quantity values
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200
[API] Follow-up cart query confirms quantity = 5
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; quantity still 2; subtotal not recalculated; HTTP 401

Title: xCart - Remove Item from Cart
Preconditions: Cart with items; lineItemId known; user authenticated
Steps:
[ACT] Execute: mutation { removeCartItem(cartId: "<CART_ID>" lineItemId: "<LINE_ITEM_ID>") { id itemsCount items { id } } }
[WAIT] Wait for response
[ASSERT] Verify errors[] present and empty
[ASSERT] Verify item removed
Assertions:
[STATE] errors[] present and empty (BL-GQL-001)
[STATE] itemsCount decremented by 1
[STATE] Removed item id no longer in items[]
[STATE] Cart remains valid (id still present)
[MATH] Totals recalculated (subTotal lower if items remain)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200
[API] Cart query confirms item absence
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; itemsCount unchanged; removed item still in items[]; cart becomes null after removal

[STATE] Shipping address saved on cart shipment

Title: xCart - Apply Coupon Mutation
Preconditions: Cart with items; valid coupon code "TESTCOUPON" configured; user authenticated
Steps:
[ACT] Execute: mutation { addCoupon(cartId: "<CART_ID>" couponCode: "TESTCOUPON") { id coupons { code isAppliedSuccessfully } totals { discountTotal { amount } } } }
[WAIT] Wait for response
[ASSERT] Verify errors[] present and empty
[ASSERT] Verify coupon applied and discount calculated
[ACT] Apply invalid coupon code "BADCODE999"
[ASSERT] Verify appropriate error returned
Assertions:
[STATE] errors[] present and empty (BL-GQL-001)
[STATE] coupons[] contains entry with code="TESTCOUPON"
[STATE] isAppliedSuccessfully = true
[MATH] totals.discountTotal.amount > 0
[STATE] Invalid coupon returns structured error or isAppliedSuccessfully = false (not a 500)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 for both calls
[API] Discount amount matches coupon configuration in Admin
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty on valid coupon; isAppliedSuccessfully = false for valid code; discountTotal = 0 after successful apply; HTTP 500 on invalid coupon
Cleanup: remove coupon from cart; delete cart

Preconditions: Cart with items, shipping address, shipping method, and payment method configured; user authenticated

[ASSERT] Verify order created with expected fields
Assertions:

[STATE] data.createOrderFromCart.id is non-empty
[FORMAT] data.createOrderFromCart.number matches order number pattern

Title: GraphQL Error Handling - Invalid Query
Preconditions: GraphQL endpoint accessible at {{BACK_URL}}; no auth required for this test
Test_Data: malformedQuery="{ products { INVALID_FIELD } }"; missingArgsQuery="mutation { createCart }"; nonExistentField="{ nonExistentTopLevelField }"
Steps:
[ACT] Send malformed query syntax (unclosed brace or invalid syntax)
[WAIT] Wait for response
[ASSERT] Verify structured GraphQL error returned (not HTTP 500)
[ACT] Send query for non-existent field
[ASSERT] Verify field-level error message
[ACT] Send mutation with missing required arguments
[ASSERT] Verify argument validation error
Assertions:
[STATE] Malformed syntax: errors[] non-empty with message describing syntax error; HTTP 200 (not 500)
[STATE] Unknown field: errors[].message references the unknown field by name
[STATE] Missing args: errors[].message references missing argument name
[FORMAT] All error responses follow GraphQL error format: { errors: [{ message, locations, path }] }
[STATE] No stack traces or internal paths in error messages
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 on all three requests (GraphQL errors via 200, not 4xx/5xx)
[CONSOLE] No unhandled server exceptions
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500 on any request; stack trace in errors[].message; no errors[] on obviously invalid query; non-JSON response body

Title: GraphQL Authentication - Unauthenticated Access
Preconditions: GraphQL endpoint accessible; products exist; auth tokens available
Test_Data: publicQuery (no token); protectedQuery (orders, no token); mutationNoToken (createCart without auth); validToken={{USER_TOKEN}}
Steps:
[ACT] Query products without Authorization header (public catalog)
[ASSERT] Verify public query succeeds
[ACT] Query orders without Authorization header (protected)
[ASSERT] Verify authentication error returned
[ACT] Execute createCart mutation without Authorization header
[ASSERT] Verify authentication error returned
[ASSERT] Verify error messages do not leak sensitive information
Assertions:
[STATE] Public products query: data.products returned; no errors[]
[STATE] Orders query without auth: errors[] non-empty with UNAUTHORIZED message (BL-GQL-004)
[STATE] createCart without auth: errors[] non-empty with UNAUTHORIZED message
[STATE] Error messages contain no user IDs, internal paths, or password hints
[FORMAT] errors[].extensions.code = "UNAUTHORIZED" or equivalent
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 on all requests (GraphQL auth errors in body, not HTTP 401 header — verify per implementation)
[API] Authenticated version of orders query succeeds
[CONSOLE] No server errors
Failure_Signals: data returned for orders/mutations without auth; HTTP 500 on unauth request; error message exposes internal details; authenticated query fails with valid token

Title: xCart - Full Checkout Flow via GraphQL
Preconditions: User authenticated; products available with known SKU; payment method configured; shipping configured for test store
Test_Data: authToken={{USER_TOKEN}}; storeId={{STORE_ID}}; productId={{TEST_SKU}}; paymentMethodCode from config
Steps:
[ACT] Create cart mutation
[ASSERT] errors[] empty; cartId returned
[ACT] Add item mutation (quantity=1)
[ASSERT] errors[] empty; itemsCount=1
[ACT] Set shipping address mutation
[ASSERT] errors[] empty; address saved
[ACT] Query available shipping methods
[ASSERT] availableShippingMethods non-empty
[ACT] Set shipping method mutation
[ASSERT] errors[] empty
[ACT] Set payment method mutation
[ASSERT] errors[] empty
[ACT] Validate cart mutation (if available)
[ACT] Create order from cart mutation
[ASSERT] errors[] empty; orderId returned
[WAIT] Measure total elapsed time
Assertions:
[STATE] Each mutation: errors[] present and empty (BL-GQL-001)
[STATE] Cart totals update correctly after each addItem/address/shipping step
[MATH] Shipping rates > 0 in availableShippingMethods
[STATE] Order created with id, number, status = "New" or "Processing"
[STATE] End-to-end flow completes in < 30 seconds total
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 on all 8 mutations
[API] Order appears in xOrder query after creation
[ADMIN] Order visible in Admin
[CONSOLE] No errors throughout flow
Failure_Signals: Any mutation returning errors[] non-empty; cart totals not updating; availableShippingMethods empty; order not created; total time > 30s
Cleanup: cancel/delete test order via Admin API

Title: GraphQL Query Performance
Test_Data: product search query; complex nested product query (variants + prices + images); orders list query; start timestamp before each query
Steps:
[ACT] Execute products search query; record start/end timestamps
[ASSERT] Response time < 500ms
[ACT] Execute complex nested product query with variants, prices, images
[ASSERT] Response time < 1000ms
[ACT] Execute orders list query
[ASSERT] Response time < 500ms
[ACT] Execute nested query 5 times; check response times do not grow proportionally (N+1 detection)
Assertions:
[STATE] Product search response time < 500ms
[STATE] Nested product query response time < 1000ms
[STATE] Orders list response time < 500ms
[STATE] Repeated nested queries: response times remain within 20% of first call (no N+1 pattern)
[STATE] No errors[] in any response
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] Monitor response times via DevTools Network panel
[CONSOLE] No timeout errors or slow query warnings
Failure_Signals: Response time exceeds threshold; response times grow linearly with nesting depth (N+1); errors[] on any query

[WAIT] Wait for response

[STATE] Each variation has prices.list.amount > 0
[STATE] Availability differs per variation if stock differs (or all show available if not tracked)

Title: GraphQL Introspection Security
Preconditions: GraphQL endpoint accessible at {{BACK_URL}}; no auth required for introspection test
Steps:
[ACT] Send introspection query: { __schema { types { name } } }
[WAIT] Wait for response
[ASSERT] Check introspection enabled/disabled status
[ASSERT] If enabled: verify no sensitive internal types exposed
Assertions:
[STATE] If introspection disabled: errors[] contains message indicating introspection not allowed (acceptable for production)
[STATE] If introspection enabled: __schema.types returned; no types with names containing "internal" "debug" "admin" "secret"
[STATE] Rate limiting applies (send 10 rapid introspection queries; no 200 on all after rate limit threshold)
[FORMAT] Response is valid JSON in either case
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 (error response) or 200 (data response); not HTTP 500
[CONSOLE] No server errors
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500 on introspection; internal type names exposed in schema; no rate limiting on repeated introspection; server crashes on introspection query

Title: xCart - Cart Uses Live Price After Admin Price Change (Not Cached Index)
Preconditions: Authenticated user; product with known price in price list; Admin API accessible at {{BACK_URL}}
Test_Data: productId={{TEST_SKU}}; priceListId from Admin; originalPrice (recorded); newPrice (different from original); authToken={{USER_TOKEN}}
Steps:
[ACT] Note current price of {{TEST_SKU}} via xCatalog products query; record as originalPrice
[ACT] Change {{TEST_SKU}} price via: PUT {{BACK_URL}}/api/pricing/pricelists/<priceListId>/prices with newPrice
[WAIT] Wait 2 seconds
[ACT] Execute addItem mutation to add {{TEST_SKU}} to a new cart
[ACT] Query cart: query { cart(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" cartId: "<CART_ID>") { items { productId listPrice { amount } placedPrice { amount } } } }
[ASSERT] Verify errors[] present and empty on all mutations
[ASSERT] Compare cart placedPrice against newPrice
Assertions:
[STATE] errors[] present and empty on createCart and addItem (BL-GQL-001)
[STATE] cart items[0].placedPrice.amount = newPrice (not originalPrice)
[STATE] cart items[0].listPrice.amount = newPrice
[STATE] No mixed pricing: all monetary fields on line item use newPrice
[STATE] BL-GQL-003: Cart resolves live price, not stale index
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 on REST price update; HTTP 200 on GraphQL mutations
[API] Price change confirmed via GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/pricing/pricelists/<priceListId>/prices
[ADMIN] Admin pricing UI reflects new price
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; placedPrice = originalPrice (stale cache); mixed pricing on line item; REST price update returns non-200
Cleanup: restore original price via PUT {{BACK_URL}}/api/pricing/pricelists/<priceListId>/prices; delete test cart

Title: xCatalog - Product Listing Price During Reindex Lag Window
Preconditions: Product with known price; Elasticsearch indexing enabled; Admin API accessible at {{BACK_URL}}
Steps:
[ACT] Query xCatalog for {{TEST_SKU}}; record listed price as originalPrice: query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" query: "<TEST_SKU_CODE>") { items { id prices { list { amount } } } } }
[ACT] Change {{TEST_SKU}} price via: PUT {{BACK_URL}}/api/pricing/pricelists/<priceListId>/prices with newPrice
[ACT] Immediately re-query xCatalog products (same query)
[ASSERT] Document whether old or new price returned (lag acceptable per BL-CROSS-002)
[WAIT] Wait 60 seconds for reindex to complete
[ACT] Re-query xCatalog products again
[ASSERT] Verify price now reflects newPrice
Assertions:
[STATE] Step 3 result: originalPrice MAY be returned (acceptable reindex lag of 30-60s)
[STATE] Step 6 result: prices.list.amount = newPrice (reindex must have completed)
[STATE] If step 6 still shows originalPrice after 90s total: flag as reindex failure
[STATE] Document actual observed lag duration
[STATE] No errors[] in any query
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 on price update REST call and GraphQL queries
[API] Reindex status checkable via {{BACK_URL}}/api/search/indexes (if endpoint available)
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: xCatalog still shows originalPrice after 90 seconds total; errors[] on xCatalog query; REST price update returns non-200; price shows as 0 after change
Cleanup: restore original price via Admin API

Title: xCart - Price Update Reflected on Re-query After Admin Change
Preconditions: Cart with existing item at known price; Admin API accessible at {{BACK_URL}}; user authenticated
Test_Data: cartId (with item); lineItemProductId={{TEST_SKU}}; originalPlacedPrice; newPrice; authToken={{USER_TOKEN}}
Steps:
[ACT] Create cart and add {{TEST_SKU}} (quantity=1) via addItem mutation
[ACT] Query cart; record items[0].placedPrice.amount as originalPlacedPrice
[ACT] Change {{TEST_SKU}} price via: PUT {{BACK_URL}}/api/pricing/pricelists/<priceListId>/prices with newPrice
[ACT] Re-query same cart: query { cart(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" cartId: "<CART_ID>") { items { productId placedPrice { amount } listPrice { amount } } totals { subTotal { amount } } } }
[ASSERT] Verify errors[] present and empty on mutations
[ASSERT] Compare placedPrice before and after price change
Assertions:
[STATE] errors[] present and empty on mutations (BL-GQL-001)
[STATE] items[0].placedPrice.amount = newPrice (not originalPlacedPrice)
[MATH] totals.subTotal.amount recalculated = newPrice × quantity
[STATE] No stale/cached price persisted in cart
[STATE] BL-GQL-003 + BL-CROSS-002: Cart uses live price regardless of index state
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 on REST update and GraphQL queries
[API] Price change confirmed via GET on pricing endpoint
[ADMIN] Admin cart view reflects new price on line item
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: items[0].placedPrice = originalPlacedPrice (P0 violation: stale cached price); subtotal not recalculated; errors[] non-empty on re-query

Title: xOrder - createOrderFromCart Idempotency (Duplicate Call Returns Same Order)
Preconditions: Full cart ready for order: items added, shipping address set, shipping method selected, payment configured; user authenticated
Steps:
[ACT] Prepare cart through full checkout flow (addItem, addOrUpdateCartShipment with address+method, addOrUpdateCartPayment)
[ACT] Execute: mutation { createOrderFromCart(cartId: "<CART_ID>") { id number status } }; record orderId1 and orderNumber1
[ASSERT] Verify errors[] present and empty
[ACT] Execute same mutation again: mutation { createOrderFromCart(cartId: "<CART_ID>") { id number status } }
[ASSERT] Verify second response
[ACT] Query orders list: query { orders(filter: "createdDate gt 2026-03-10") { totalCount items { id number } } }
[ASSERT] Verify exactly 1 order created
Assertions:
[STATE] errors[] present and empty on first call (BL-GQL-001)
[STATE] First call: orderId1 and orderNumber1 non-empty
[STATE] Second call: either (a) returns same orderId1/orderNumber1, or (b) returns structured GraphQL error with clear message (NOT HTTP 500)
[STATE] orders query: totalCount for this cart = 1 (not 2)
[STATE] No duplicate payment charges; no duplicate inventory decrements
[STATE] BL-CROSS-010: All checkout mutations must be idempotent
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 on both mutation calls (even if second returns errors[])
[API] Exactly 1 order in system for this cart
[ADMIN] Single order visible in Admin orders list
[CONSOLE] No unhandled exceptions on second call
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500 on second call; 2 orders created; duplicate payment charge; errors[] on first (successful) call; second call returns different orderId
Cleanup: cancel/delete test order via Admin API; clean up test cart

Title: xOrder - Second createOrderFromCart Returns Same Order ID (Not Server Error)
Preconditions: Full cart ready for order creation; user authenticated
Steps:
[ACT] Prepare cart through full checkout flow
[ACT] Execute createOrderFromCart mutation; record orderId and orderNumber
[WAIT] Wait 2 seconds
[ACT] Execute createOrderFromCart with same cartId again
[ASSERT] Inspect response structure of second call
[ASSERT] If successful response: verify orderId matches first call
[ASSERT] If error response: verify structured GraphQL error (not HTTP 500)
Assertions:
[STATE] First call: errors[] present and empty; valid orderId returned (BL-GQL-001)
[STATE] Second call: MUST NOT return HTTP 500 or unhandled exception
[STATE] Acceptable second-call responses: (a) same orderId, or (b) errors[].message = "Cart already converted" or "Cart not found" or equivalent
[STATE] errors[] if present on second call: has message, locations, path — no stack trace
[STATE] No partial order created by second call

Title: xCart - Cart Invalidated After Order Creation (No Reuse)
Preconditions: Cart converted to order via createOrderFromCart; orderId known; user authenticated
Test_Data: cartId (converted to order); orderId; productId={{TEST_SKU}}; authToken={{USER_TOKEN}}
Steps:
[ACT] Create cart, add items, complete checkout setup
[ACT] Execute createOrderFromCart mutation; record orderId
[ASSERT] Verify errors[] present and empty; order created
[ACT] Attempt to add item to same converted cart: mutation { addItem(cartId: "<CART_ID>" productId: "{{TEST_SKU}}" quantity: 1) { id itemsCount } }
[ASSERT] Verify addItem fails with appropriate error
[ACT] Query cart: query { cart(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" cartId: "<CART_ID>") { id status itemsCount } }
[ASSERT] Verify cart inactive/null
[ACT] Attempt changeCartItemQuantity on converted cart
[ASSERT] Verify mutation fails
Assertions:
[STATE] createOrderFromCart: errors[] empty; valid orderId (BL-GQL-001)
[STATE] addItem on converted cart: returns error (cart not found / cart already converted / similar); NOT silent success
[STATE] Cart query: returns null or cart.status indicates inactive/completed
[STATE] changeCartItemQuantity: returns error, not success
[STATE] BL-CROSS-010: Post-order cart invalidated to prevent reuse and duplicate orders
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 on all calls (errors in body for failures)
[API] No new line items on order after attempted addItem
[ADMIN] Cart not modifiable in Admin after order creation
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: Silent success on addItem to converted cart (P0 violation); cart still shows active status; mutations succeed on converted cart

Title: xCart - Currency Switch Updates All Line Items to New Price List
Preconditions: Products with prices in both USD and EUR price lists; authenticated user; changeCartCurrency mutation available
Test_Data: cartId; productA_id (has USD+EUR prices); productB_id (has USD+EUR prices); authToken={{USER_TOKEN}}
Steps:
[ACT] Create cart in USD: mutation { createCart(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" userId: "<USER_ID>" currencyCode: "USD" cultureName: "en-US") { id } }
[ACT] Add productA and productB via addItem mutations
[ASSERT] errors[] empty on each mutation
[ACT] Query cart; record USD prices: items[].listPrice, items[].placedPrice, totals.subTotal
[ACT] Switch currency: mutation { changeCartCurrency(cartId: "<CART_ID>" newCurrencyCode: "EUR") { id currency { code } items { productId listPrice { amount currencyCode } placedPrice { amount currencyCode } } totals { subTotal { amount currencyCode } } } }
[ASSERT] Verify errors[] present and empty
[ASSERT] Verify ALL monetary fields use EUR
Assertions:
[STATE] errors[] present and empty on changeCartCurrency (BL-GQL-001)
[STATE] All items[].listPrice.currencyCode = "EUR"
[STATE] All items[].placedPrice.currencyCode = "EUR"
[STATE] totals.subTotal.currencyCode = "EUR"
[STATE] Price amounts correspond to EUR price list values (NOT USD-to-EUR conversion rates)
[STATE] Zero fields showing USD after currency switch (atomic update — BL-CROSS-004)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] HTTP 200 on all mutations
[API] EUR prices match Admin price list configuration for EUR
[CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty on currency switch; any item still showing currencyCode="USD"; price appears to be USD amount with EUR label; totals.subTotal in wrong currency

[CONSOLE] No errors
